name: El Celler de Can Roca
recognition: 3 Michelin stars; World's 50 Best No.1 in 2013 & 2015 (then retired to Best of the Best)
what_to_order: The full tasting: caramelised olives plucked from a bonsai, closing on Jordi's perfume-built desserts.
reservation: Books ~11 months ahead, midnight on the 1st of the month; card to hold
best_time: Lunch — marginally easier to land than dinner
notes: Closed Sun & Mon; the booking window, not the walk, is the gatekeeper
verdict: Forty years in, all three brothers are still on the floor — this is not a three-star coasting on its two world titles.
caveat: Bookings open at midnight on the 1st of each month, eleven months out, card to hold; shut Sunday and Monday, and 16 August–1 September this summer.
iconic_orders: Menú Festival — the full tasting — The only menu — appetiser cavalcade, twelve courses, three desserts; olives to perfume.; Olives caramelitzades — the bonsai olives — Anchovy-stuffed caramelised olives hung glinting from a bonsai olive tree.; El món (The World) — Five one-bite world flavours on arms extending from a globe; Peru, Turkey recur.; Gamba de Palamós marinada — Rice-vinegar-marinated red prawn, head-juice sauce, crisp fried legs, phytoplankton brioche.; Adaptation of Eternity by Calvin Klein — Bergamot, basil, tangerine, vanilla — the fragrance rebuilt edible, a line since 2001.; Josep's wine pairing — Optional €155 pairing; cellar built as five wine 'chapels' — Champagne, Burgundy, Riesling, Priorat, Jerez.

name: Restaurant Massana
recognition: 1 Michelin star, first awarded 2007, held through the 2026 Guide; fourth-generation kitchen
what_to_order: Homenaje al Magret de Pato — duck breast off the grill, on the card since 1986.
notes: Quieter than Can Roca but still needs advance booking
verdict: Girona's longest-held star after Can Roca's — kept since 2007, and bookable in days, not seasons.
caveat: Shut Sunday and Monday, last tasting-menu seating at nine sharp — book days ahead, not on the night.
iconic_orders: Homenatge al Magret d'Ànec Massana (1986) — Grill-charred duck breast, herb oil, rosemary pear — never off the card since 1986.; Caneló de pularda, escamarlà i crema de ceba dolça — Silky poularde caneló under langoustine and sweet-onion cream — Catalan classic, starred.; Ceps, gambes i tòfona — Autumn only — raw cep carpaccio, marinated prawns, truffle, pine-nut vinaigrette.; Ou estrellat — Trompe-l'oeil egg of yoghurt, mango and white chocolate — theatrical finish.

name: Divinum
recognition: 1 Michelin star, held in the 2026 Guide; kitchen trained under Joan Roca
what_to_order: The classic steak tartare, mustard-cool, with whatever Laura pours against it.
notes: Below street level in the Casino building — easy to walk past
verdict: The only star in Girona won from the dining room, not the pass — Joan and Laura's floor, since the wine-bar days.
caveat: Shut Sunday and Monday, kitchen closes at ten — and the vault sits below street level, easy to march straight past.
iconic_orders: Classic beef steak tartare — Carved and sauced in the room; crowned Spain's best tartare 2022; €38.50.; Pèsols del Maresme — Spring only — pil-pil broth, cod tripe, truffle shavings; Michelin's named standout.; Menú Essència with Laura Tejero's pairing — €90 pairing over the €160 menu — the sommelier-led room's whole point.; The cheese trolley (selecció de formatges) — Twenty-plus wheels — Rey Silo blue, Gargòla del Miracle — €25 to finish.; Chocolate and walnuts — Casadevall's chocolate slot — his aubergine-Valrhona 'Horta dolça' won Best Chocolate Dessert 2022.; Fig-leaf flan ('Figs leaf flam') — House flan, fig-leaf nitro ice cream churned tableside, carob crumble; €15.

name: Normal
recognition: The Roca brothers' deliberately informal room; interiors by Andreu Carulla
what_to_order: The whisky tart with egg-yolk ice cream — the one dessert the inspectors single out.
notes: The companion Bar Normal opens at noon for vermouth and all-day picking
verdict: The Rocas at their most Catalan — their mother's home cooking by El Celler-trained hands, not a three-star spin-off doing tricks.
caveat: Shut Mondays; the diary opens 90 days ahead at midnight, card down — €50 a head if you cancel inside three days.
iconic_orders: Truita Sacha amb carpaccio de gamba vermella — Half-set omelette under raw Palamós prawn carpaccio — homage to Madrid's Sacha.; Filet Wellington de vedella madurada — Dry-aged beef in glossy pastry, salsa cafè París — the other must-order.; Tarta al whisky amb gelat de rovell d'ou — Since day one — boozy retro gateau, egg-yolk ice cream; Michelin names it.; Croqueta de llet d'ovella Mas Marcè i pernil ibèric — Raw ripollesa ewe's-milk béchamel in a whisper-thin crust; ordered by the piece.; Els arrossos — Repsol-tipped rices; this carta, dry rice of perol sausage and baby cuttlefish.; Patates fregides amb maionesa de tòfona i formatge (Bar Normal) — The weeks-old companion bar's calling card — crisp chips, truffle mayonnaise, cheese.

name: Casa Cacao
recognition: Jordi Roca + Anna Payet's bean-to-bar house and 15-room hotel over the Onyar
what_to_order: The thick hot chocolate — single-origin, conched metres away, dense enough to coat the spoon.
notes: Shop is walk-in; the 15-room hotel above books out fast
verdict: Not a three-star gift shop — Jordi Roca roasts, grinds and conches the beans on the premises, and the cup proves it.
caveat: A café, not a lunch room — the bookable brunch is the hotel roof's midday sitting (12:00–14:00, about €35, reserve ahead); the shop is walk-in, the fifteen rooms above are not.
iconic_orders: Xocolata calenta (thick hot chocolate) — House-roasted, conched metres away; thickened a la taza — dense enough to coat the spoon; Xuixo Casa Cacao — Girona's fried pastry, warm, filled with 72% Colombian Arhuaco chocolate cream; Fartonne — Elongated panettone-dough brioche, made for dunking in the hot chocolate; Single-origin bars (Hacienda Victoria 68%, Coop. Norandino 70%, Finca La Rioja 75%) — Ecuador, Piura and Chiapas lots, wrapped in paper made from their cacao husks; Brunch a La Terrassa — €55 rooftop sitting over the old town; seasonal, small-producer spread

name: Rocambolesc
recognition: Jordi Roca's gelateria — the Celler's dessert language at street level
what_to_order: The Panet — brioche pressed hot around cold soft-serve; crackling crust, frozen heart.
notes: Queues form fast in summer; a candy confiteria shares the address
verdict: Three-Michelin-star pastry thinking at four euros a cone — and the queue down Santa Clara knows it.
caveat: Walk-in only, and shut by 21:00 five nights a week (23:00 Friday–Saturday) — no midweek after-dinner cone.
iconic_orders: Panet — Brioche run hot through the 'Oxymoron Maker'; crackling crust, cold soft-serve heart.; Helado de manzana al horno (baked-apple soft-serve) — House classic — baked-apple cream under caramelised apple and buttery biscuit crumbs.; Rocanas (the nose) — Strawberry-and-rosewater lolly cast from a 3D scan of Jordi's own nose.; Mano Dorada (the Golden Hand) — Jaime Lannister's gilt hand — blood orange, mango, cocoa butter, gold-dusted.

name: Can Marquès
recognition: One of Girona's longest-running family tables, opened 1920, now fourth generation
what_to_order: The Thursday rice — the pan carries whatever the market gave up that morning.
reservation: Advisable for the three dinner nights
notes: Night service Wed–Fri only; it shops the market next door
verdict: Four unbroken generations since 1920 — among Girona's longest-running tables, and the Mercat del Lleó next door still sets the menu.
caveat: Lunch is the meal: dinner is Thursday and Friday only, groups only — and Saturdays shut mid-June to mid-September.
iconic_orders: L'arròs del dijous (the Thursday rice) — Thursday is rice day — market-driven arròs, the Mercat del Lleó across the square.; Botifarra de sal i pebre esparracada amb formatge brie — Salt-and-pepper botifarra torn open under melting brie — old-school market comfort.; Bacallà amb samfaina i romesco — Salt cod under slow-stewed samfaina, finished with nut-thick romesco.; Pota i tripa de vedella — Trotter and tripe in one gelatinous stew — proper market-day offal.; Llengua de vedella estofada amb bolets — Veal tongue braised with mushrooms until it yields to a spoon.; Escudella i carn d'olla (winter Wednesdays) — Winter Wednesdays only — steaming broth first, then the platter of boiled meats.

name: Casa Marieta
neighborhood: Barri Vell · Plaça de la Independència
recognition: On the arcaded square since 1892, five generations in
what_to_order: Duck with pears: the old Catalan way, pears gone amber in the roasting juices.
notes: Also does takeaway if the room is full
verdict: Girona's oldest restaurant — on a square ringed with look-alike terraces, the one that has cooked here since 1892 and still cooks like it.
caveat: Open daily but on rails — lunch 12.30 to 3.30, dinner 7.30 to 10.30, half an hour longer at weekends, nothing between; the arcaded terrace books out first, and nobody here is chasing tasting-menu invention.
iconic_orders: Ànec amb peres — The old Catalan way — pears turned amber in the duck's roasting juices.; Canelons Casa Marieta — The grandmother's canelons — the house sells them under its own name.; Espatlla de xai al forn — Baked whole till the meat loosens from the bone — a listed house classic.; Sípia amb pèsols — Cuttlefish dice simmered with sweet peas — sea-and-garden Catalan standard.; Xuixo de Girona — Girona's cream-filled fried pastry, sugar-crusted — made in-house, press calls it legendary.

name: Fonda Cal Ros
neighborhood: Barri Vell · Carrer de la Cort Reial
address: Carrer de la Cort Reial 9, 17004 Girona
product_tags: Stone vaults; Els Tinars team; Seasonal
recognition: Historic inn relaunched by the Els Tinars / Marc Gascons team
what_to_order: Rockfish rice a la llauna — baked flat in its tin until the edges catch.
notes: Closed Tue; on the same street where the xuixo was invented
verdict: Not the Gascons' starred table — the same Els Tinars hands cooking fricandó and rice a la llauna for fonda money under the old inn's vaults.
caveat: Shut Tuesdays and Wednesdays — not the Monday you'd guess; plates land to share, so order as a table, not as individuals.
iconic_orders: Arròs a la llauna de peix de roca, gambetes i escamarlans — Baked flat in its tin — rockfish, small prawns, langoustines; edges catch crisp.; Arbre d'embotits artesans — Charcuterie hung on a table-side tree; Torroella de Montgrí cuts, sold by weight.; Canelons de rostit tradicional — Roast-meat cannelloni ben gratinats — béchamel blistered deep and dark.; Porró de vi — Empordà vi de pagès drunk from the glass porró, passed round the table.; Crema catalana tradicional cremada — The traditional burnt crust — a thin caramel lid, cracked with the spoon.

name: Nexe
recognition: The old Nu, transferred to its own chefs in Aug 2022; MICHELIN Guide selection + Guía Repsol Recomendado 2026
what_to_order: Steak tartare, worked up in front of you at the open bar.
notes: Nu's old domain is dead; don't bank on the tasting menu third-party sites advertise
verdict: Nu is gone — Massana signed the room over to his own cooks in 2022, and as Nexe they've kept their MICHELIN Guide listing without him.
caveat: Shut Sunday and Monday, two-hour service windows — book, and don't bank on the tasting menu TheFork advertises; the carte runs short.
iconic_orders: Steak tàrtar de vaca charolesa — Forty-day dry-aged Charolais chopped at the open bar; smoked eel, raisins, pine nuts; Ostra Gillardeau amb salsa ponzu — Gillardeau No 2 opened to order under ponzu and seven-spice — on since 2023; Canelons — House caneló, refilled by season — truffled Mas el Cros black chicken, now morel sauce; Xuixo — Girona's fried pastry made to order — àvia Núria's crema catalana, caramel ice cream; Arròs sec — Empordà dry rice, garnish rotating — Palamós prawn veil once, botifarra negra now

name: Cal Formatger
neighborhood: Barri Vell · Carrer de la Cort Reial
address: Carrer de la Cort Reial 12, 17001 Girona
recognition: A cheese room built on Girona-province makers — Garrotxa goat's cheese front and centre
what_to_order: The Girona-comarca board, cut to order — start with the grey-rinded, hazelnut-sweet goat's cheeses.
reservation: Walk-in to shop; ask ahead for a private tasting
notes: Small room, opens 10:30; closed Sun & Mon
verdict: The quesería Guía Repsol bills as Joan Roca's favourite in his own city — proof a province of small dairies can carry a counter alone.
caveat: Shut Sunday and Monday, and the counter goes dark mid-afternoon most days; tastings want notice, not a walk-in.
iconic_orders: Les Safates — the cut-to-order cheese board — Four sizes, Parella €25 to XXL €55 — 'acadèmicament tallats', laid with grace.; Puigpedrós (Molí de Ger) — Pere Pujol's washed-rind raw cow from Ger — orange rind, Munster-like, intensity 5/5.; Glauc (Formatgeria Xauxa, Les Preses) — Paula Fonollà's creamy raw-milk blue from Les Preses — Lactium 2021 best Catalan cheese.; Tou del Lluçanès — Their 'torta de vaca a la catalana' — spoon-soft raw cow's cheese, €15.; Cervesa Minera — Craft beer brewed in Sant Joan de les Abadesses — full Minera catalogue stocked.; Audio tast premiats — guided tasting pack — Cheese packs with the shop's audio-guided tastings — at home, or in-shop with makers.

name: Arròs 9 by Terram
recognition: The casual rice-focused offshoot of the gastronomic Terram, same chef
what_to_order: Arròs del senyoret amb llagosta — lobster rice, everything peeled, spoon straight to the crust.
notes: Rice dishes usually need two — call ahead if solo
verdict: Not a photo-menu paella stop — Terram's chef directs the rice pans, and each arròs is built on its own sofregit.
caveat: Shut Wednesdays, and dinner only four nights a week — Sundays and Tuesdays it's lunch or nothing.
iconic_orders: Arròs del senyorit, de bogavante — sec — Peeled lobster rice, nothing to shell — spoon straight down to the socarrat; minimum two.; Arròs sec de porc ibèric flambejat — Grill-soaked grains, flambéed presa ibèrica; reviewers singled out the morcilla alioli.; Arròs negre sec — Squid-ink dry rice with langoustines and saffron lactonesa — scrape the toasted crust.; Croquetes de rostit tradicional — Three-roast-meat croquettes, molten centre, ordered by the piece.; Xuixo de Girona flambejat al rom — Girona's cream-filled pastry from Can Castelló, rum-flambéed, with vanilla ice cream.

name: Can Castelló
recognition: Brands itself the keeper of the early xuixo recipe (its 116-year Santa Clara flagship closed 2014)
what_to_order: The classic crema xuixo, eaten warm — sugar crust cracking into soft crema catalana.
notes: Argenteria 7 is the only Girona address — the Calderers counter is gone; the 'best xuixo' title is contested (Tornés won the 2026 world contest)
verdict: Not the 2026 world-champion xuixo — Tornés took that — but the only house on earth frying nothing else.
caveat: A branded flagship, not the vanished old bakery — the frying happens in Banyoles, the Calderers counter is gone, and Argenteria 7 is the only address.
iconic_orders: Xuixo de Crema — The classic, warm — crackling sugar crust into soft egg-rich crema; their best-seller.; Xuixo de Crema de Ratafia — Cream cold-infused with ratafia herbs, finished with the Catalan liqueur itself.; Xuixo de Cabell d'Àngel — Silky candied-pumpkin strands inside the fried, sugar-rolled lamination.

name: La Fàbrica
neighborhood: Barri Vell · Carrer de la Llebre
recognition: Girona's first third-wave café, founded by a pro cyclist and his wife — hub of the city's riding scene
what_to_order: The brunch board with a filter coffee — beans they roast themselves at Espresso Mafia.
reservation: None — expect a wait at peak brunch
notes: Open daily 9:00–17:00, incl. Sundays — useful when the rest is shut
verdict: Girona's first third-wave café — the 2015 original the city's whole cycling-coffee scene grew out of.
caveat: No bookings, a wall of Lycra at weekend brunch, and doors shut at five — dinner is elsewhere.
iconic_orders: Flat white — Double shot under foamed milk — beans Meier roasts himself at Espresso Mafia; Nordic bagel — Herb cream cheese laid on thick, smoked salmon, shaved cucumber; Fruity Forest — Violet-sugar yoghurt ringed by a kaleidoscope of tropical fruit; Cafè amb gel — Order iced espresso, cold brew arrives — Meier refuses to ice-shock his roasts; Pulled pork bagel — Tender pulled pork, melted cheese, bright mango salsa — post-ride fuel

name: Mercat del Lleó
recognition: The working municipal market, in a purpose-built 1944 hall of ~1,712 m² and ~60 stalls
what_to_order: Day-boat Costa Brava fish off the Salvador counter — bright-eyed, stiff, smelling of the sea.
best_time: 07:00–10:00 for the best fish and produce
notes: Mon–Fri 07:00–14:00, Sat 07:00–14:30; closed Sun. A locals' market, not a tourist stop
verdict: The 1944 hall Girona still cooks from — a working larder, not a food-hall conversion.
caveat: Shut Sundays and wound down by two; after ten the fish is picked over — come to shop, not to graze.
iconic_orders: Peix de costa — Peixateria Salvador (parades 74–75, 91–93) — Llançà, Roses and Blanes day-boats — bright-eyed, stiff, smelling of clean sea; Botifarra dolça — Empordà's sweet pork sausage — sugar and lemon peel; fry with Girona apple; Poma de Girona (IGP) — Sea-tempered plain-grown apples — Gala, Golden, Granny Smith; EU PGI since 2003; Bacallà — Món Salat (parades 78–80) — Top-grade salt cod plus brandada, piquillos, olives, bunyols de bacallà; Formatges de Mas Alba — El Petit Productor (parades 56–59) — The Vilademuls farm's own stall — seven goat cheeses; try Babaus, Petitot; Salsafins — October-to-April Girona root — sweet, delicate; classically stewed with duck

name: Espelt Viticultors
recognition: One of the largest DO Empordà producers, farming organically on the Alt Empordà
what_to_order: Airam: grandfather Lluís's solera Garnatxa — gold shading to red, every vintage since 1997.
notes: Province, not city — an hour out; the town coordinate is approximate
verdict: The big estate that farms like a small one — 200 organic hectares of DO Empordà with a solera in the cellar running back to 1997.
caveat: Book and pay online first — the 11:00 vineyard tours run on set dates only (weekly in August, thin the rest of the year), €27–38 a head, and it's a good fifty minutes from Girona; make it a Cap de Creus morning, not a detour.
iconic_orders: Airam — Grandfather Lluís's solera since 1997 — gold-to-red; rosemary honey, nuts, cinnamon.; Coma Bruna — Century-old Carinyena on Rabós slate — plum, truffle; Decanter 94 (Evans).; Sauló — The soulful young Garnatxa–Carinyena — violets, rosemary, garriga herbs; Vinari Gold 2021.; La Vella — Near-extinct Carinyena Blanca, century vines, cement-egg lees — hazelnut, orange peel.; Garnatxa de l'Empordà (50 cl) — Bright-amber natural sweet Garnatxa — cherry, honey, herbs; half-bottle for the galley.; Vinagre de Garnatxa — Garnatxa-only wine vinegar — blackberry-sharp; a teaspoon wakes stews and braises.

name: Esperit Roca
neighborhood: La Fortalesa · Sant Julià de Ramis (10 km N)
address: Castell de Sant Julià de Ramis, Muntanya dels Sants Metges, Carrer Major s/n, 17481 Sant Julià de Ramis, Girona
recognition: 1 MICHELIN star, first awarded Guide España 2025, retained 2026; the Roca brothers' fort project (opened May 2024)
what_to_order: Toda la gamba — Palamós red prawn marinated in seaweed vinegar and katsuobushi; prawn velouté, sauce of the head, crisp legs, citric caviar, a concentrated distillate of the coral.
price_range: €€€€ — Salty Spirit menu €170, Sweet Spirit menu €139, à la carte available
reservation: Books 90 days out; EUR 50 pp late-cancellation fee
best_time: Lunch (Fri-Mon), for the views over Girona
notes: Closed Tue-Wed; dinner Thu-Sat only; holiday closure 3-11 Nov 2026
verdict: This is El Celler's back catalogue served under its own Michelin star — bookable ninety days out at €170, where the mothership takes eleven months and some €145 more.
caveat: Not El Celler and not pretending to be — this is the archive, refined, not the frontier. You need a car or taxi up the hill from Girona; Tuesdays and Wednesdays are dark; dinner runs only Thursday to Saturday; and €139–170 before wine is real money for revival dishes.
iconic_orders: Toda la gamba (2012) — Palamós red prawn — seaweed-vinegar marinade, head sauce, crisp legs, coral distillate.; Mar y montaña vegetal (2007) — Plankton mousse, seaweeds, salicornia, enoki — surf-and-turf rendered wholly in vegetables.; Brioche de pularda — Poularde brioche with a crackling, Pithiviers-like crust — on the Salty Spirit menu.; Libro viejo (2017) — Jordi Roca's enfleurage old-book scent over Earl Grey cream, madeleine ice cream, mille-feuille.; Ginebra de Té de Roca — House-distilled rock-tea gin — Jasonia glutinosa on Penedès-wine spirit; made to sip neat.

name: Can Roca
recognition: El Celler de Can Roca's staff eat their daily lunch here — Joan Roca sends his three-star brigade, seventy-plus strong, to his parents' dining room between services.
what_to_order: The menú's fish day — rap a la marinera, monkfish yielding under a dense marinera sauce that asks for bread.
notes: Mon–Fri: bar 6:30–17:00, fork breakfasts 8:30–10:30, menú 13:00–15:30 in two sittings. Closed Sat–Sun.
verdict: The cheapest sit-down meal in the Roca universe — €18 buys lunch from the family kitchen that raised a three-Michelin-star dynasty, and the Celler's own brigade eats it daily.
caveat: A formica-and-telly neighbourhood bar half an hour's walk from the Barri Vell, weekday lunch only — go for the cooking and the story, not a sighting of the brothers; the menú has crept from €16 (2023) via €17 (spring 2025) to €18 (autumn 2025), and August closing is unverified, so ring 972 20 51 19 before crossing town.
iconic_orders: Menú del dia — A short slate of firsts and seconds rotating daily — spoon dishes and seasonal stews up front, rice on its appointed day; €18, autumn 2025.; Rap a la marinera — Monkfish fresh and yielding in a dense marinera that asks for bread — documented on the menú as far back as 2016.; Calamars a la romana — Doña Montse's own recipe, ordered as an extra over the menú (€10.50, 2023) — a light fry, crisp jacket, the squid tender beneath.; Esmorzar de forquilla — Fork breakfast, 8:30–10:30 — the working-Girona ritual of stew before ten, added to the bar's slate in 2024.; Postres casolans — Crema catalana, arròs amb llet, baked apple, lemon mousse — homemade closers; the Rocambolesc gelat on the counter is a 2016 sighting.

name: Dit i Fet
recognition: Joan Roca names it his place to eat in Girona — 'run by people who had worked with us' (Infobae, 2025).
what_to_order: ★ Tortilla cremosa de gamba de Palamós — a barely-set omelette running with Palamós red prawn, the coast folded into eggs (€24).
verdict: Joan Roca's named pick to eat out in Girona — ex-Celler craft at €40–50 a head, and the 2026 Michelin Guide agrees it belongs on the map.
caveat: Closed Tuesday and Wednesday and the secret is out — reserve. At €40–50 a head it sits at the dear end of 'casual', and the short seasonal card means a dish you read about may have rotated off.
iconic_orders: Tortilla cremosa de gamba de Palamós — Custard-loose centre carrying the sweet iodine of Palamós red prawn — €24, and one of the two dishes the Michelin inspectors single out.; Croquetes de rabo — Slow-braised tail meat under a shattering crumb — billed as Girona veal-tail in Infobae's 2025 write-up, listed as oxtail croquettes on the current menu.; Tartar de vieira, oliva manzanilla i piparra — Scallop tartare with manzanilla olive and piparra sauce — the 2026 Michelin inspectors' other named pick.; Cap i pota — Old-Girona stew of head and trotter, €19.50 on the July 2026 card — offal cookery with Celler discipline; not for the timid.; Xuixo a la brasa — La Puntual's cream-filled xuixo — Girona's own pastry — charred over the grill with vanilla ice cream melting against warm caramelised sugar; €7.50 of terroir as dessert.

name: SAFO
neighborhood: Barri Vell — by Plaça del Vi
recognition: Joan Roca: 'Not only our friends and neighbours — very good natural wines. And you eat very well' (Infobae, 2025).
what_to_order: ★ Crispy octopus quesadilla with avocado cream — Richter's Mexico–Catalunya axis in a single bite.
notes: Jul–Aug: Mon–Fri 19:00–24:00, dinner only · rest of year: Tue 20:00–24:00, Wed–Sat 13:00–16:00 & 20:00–24:00; kitchen 13:00–15:30 / 20:00–22:00 (official site, 2026)
verdict: Girona's most serious natural-wine room wearing its least serious face — vouched for, in print, by the three-star neighbour up the street.
caveat: July–August is dinner only, Monday–Friday — and the one-page menu changes constantly, so the dish you read about may be gone. Natural wine means funk: trust Víctor rather than asking for Rioja.
iconic_orders: Crispy octopus quesadilla, avocado cream — Griddle-blistered tortilla, tender octopus, cool avocado — the house's two homelands on one plate.; Stracciatella galette with grilled bimi — Milky stracciatella against grilled bimi, chopped nuts and sea fennel — though the galette's topping turns with the market week to week.; Natural wine by the glass — Víctor Martín's low-intervention cellar, Catalan and beyond — he learned the genre at Barcelona's Bar Brutal; say what you like, let him pour.

name: Nomo Girona
neighborhood: Barri Vell — Pujada de la Mercè
recognition: Opening covered by El Punt Avui (22 October 2021) — the 'emblematic' Jardins de la Mercè manor, with its protected and officially catalogued romantic garden, reopened by the Catalan-Japanese group, chef-partner Naoyuki Haginoya named at the stoves.
what_to_order: Menú Naoyuki — the chef-partner's fourteen-course €50 set run, truffled edamame through yuzu mochi
notes: Lunch 13:30–17:00, dinner 20:00–24:00 — but aggregators disagree on days (daily vs closed Monday with Sunday lunch only) and the group site publishes none; confirm on +34 972 22 68 45
verdict: A group outpost that out-sites the town's independents — dining rooms wrapped round a protected, city-catalogued 1900 romantic garden, with a Tokyo-formed partner-chef writing the menu.
caveat: It is the tenth opening of a twelve-strong Catalan group, not a chef-owner's only room — the same Naoyuki menu runs in Barcelona and Madrid, and it holds no Michelin or Repsol mention; here you are paying, in part, for the garden.
iconic_orders: Menú Naoyuki (€50) — Fourteen courses — the chef-partner's whole argument in one sitting: truffled fried edamame, sukiyaki croquette, gyu-niku gyoza, ebi chili — izakaya comfort run through Mediterranean product.; Sukiyaki croquette — Japanese-style oxtail croqueta under hard panko — molten, beefy centre; the house's Catalan-Tokyo handshake in one bite.; Ebi chili — Spicy prawns with fried egg — Haginoya's Tokyo comfort register, closer to izakaya than sushi counter.; Butter-fish nigiri — Silky and fat-rich, cut with kimchi sauce and pickled piparras — on the current Naoyuki card.; Yuzu mochi with white chocolate — The set's current close — citrus sharpness in a soft rice skin; the card rotates, so expect movement here.

name: El Cul de la Lleona x Catmandu
what_to_order: Curry de pollo nepalí — the plate the house itself flags as its most-loved
verdict: The only Nepali kitchen we could verify inside the Barri Vell — worth the detour for momos beside the lioness gironins kiss to come home, not for fine-dining polish.
caveat: No bylined review or guide mention exists — the signal here is the place, not the press; dinner is Thursday to Saturday only, the city's roster still carries a stale pre-rebrand tapas listing, and historians note the lioness-kissing rite is itself a later embellishment — kissed daily all the same.
iconic_orders: Mo:mo (5 u, €8–12) — Nepali dumplings five to a plate, veg or meat, steamed or fried — the C-momo version arrives in a tomato-vegetable sauce; the litmus test of any Nepali kitchen.; Curry de pollo nepalí — Slow, spice-deep, with basmati rice — the house's own site calls it the most appreciated plate on the card, and it anchors the lunch menú too.; Dal bhat (veg €12 / chicken €14.50) — Nepal's national plate in full — curry, lentil soup, basmati and potato salad on one tray; order it and eat like Kathmandu.; Vedella a la cassola — Beef stewed in wine and beer with chimichurri and fried vegetables — the Catalan half of the marriage, pure menú-del-dia soul.; Craft beer from the fridge — Artisan and international bottles chosen to stand up to spice — this is still, at heart, a Girona bar.

name: König 2
recognition: Founded 5 February 1973 by Pere Parals after a trip to Germany; Grup König marked 50 years and its 12th restaurant in 2023 (Caternews Digital); konig.cat lists eleven as of 2026.
what_to_order: ★ Patates braves amb salsa König — the plate that outsells every drink on the till
notes: Daily 09:30–23:00, Fri–Sat until 23:30 (konig.cat, checked July 2026)
verdict: Bravas, a frankfurt and a canya on the terrace will come in under €15 — in the city of El Celler de Can Roca, the people's institution still feeds you for under €10 (Ara, 2024). If your bill says otherwise, you ordered the 200 g premium burger.
caveat: It's a local chain of eleven branches now (konig.cat, July 2026) — including Barcelona and a shopping-centre outlet — and the original 1973 room was demolished after closing in December 2014. You're visiting the institution's heir, not a time capsule: paper mats, families, speed. Come for the bravas and the square, not for terroir romance.
iconic_orders: Patates braves — Peeled and cut by hand that morning — thousands of kilos daily across the group — fried to a hard rustle and gloved in the house orange sauce: creamy, gently hot, warmer than allioli, politer than a Madrid brava. The dish that outsells every drink on the menu (Ara, 2024).; Frankfurt — The 1973 founding order — a snap-skinned German-style sausage of Catalan production in a crusty roll with mustard. Nothing clever, which is the point.; Hamburguesa 10 — Fried egg, bacon, cheese and pink sauce collapsing into one another — 'molt contundent' by Ara's own 2024 warning, and the students' order of record.; König 1973 lager — The house-badged beer — cold, blameless, built to chase bravas (konig.cat lists it as the group's own brand).

name: Zanpanzar
address: Carrer de la Cort Reial, 10-12, 17004 Girona (tel. 972 212 843)
what_to_order: ★ Hot txistorra pintxo off the passing tray — scarlet Basque sausage, its paprika fat soaking the bread beneath
notes: Evenings daily ~19:00–24:00 (Mon to 23:30 per listings); Sat–Sun also midday service (~12:00–16:00). Post-handover hours drift — ring ahead.
verdict: Walk in at 23:15 and the trays should still be circulating — that is the whole case for the card; if the spread is down to three tired sticks, the new regime has failed the one job the old one never did.
caveat: Midnight is the hard stop, and the handover is recent — the room and pintxos survived the May-2025 change of hands but the founding owners did not; arrive by 23:00 and treat the ~€2.50-a-stick figure as indicative, to be confirmed at the bar.
iconic_orders: Txistorra pintxo — hot off the plancha, garlicky and scarlet, the fat staining the bread — the counter's loudest argument; Tortilla pintxo — cut thick from the omelette, centre just set — the staple you judge any pintxo bar by; Bacallà amb piperrada — the new team's addition — cod over slow-stewed peppers, Basque to the bone; Pulpo a la gallega — Galician-style octopus under pimentón and oil — one of the held-over classics from the first 25 years; Cachopo — the Asturian interloper — breaded veal folded round ham and cheese; a post-service feed if ever there was one; Patatas bravas — kept through the handover — order them when the pintxo trays slow after 23:00

name: Restaurant 8de7
recognition: Holds a Guía Repsol Solete — the guide's marker for honest, sunny neighbourhood spots.
what_to_order: Patates braves 8de7 — the house's own reinvention and standing dare: 'no n'heu vist cap d'iguals' — you will not have seen their like.
notes: Lunch Tue–Sat 13:15–16:30 (kitchen to 15:30); dinner Tue–Sat 21:00–24:00 (kitchen to 23:00). Closed Sun–Mon.
verdict: The best-value serious cooking within walking distance of the Rambla — a Repsol-Solete kitchen at canteen money, which is why its few tables are spoken for by half one.
caveat: A small room and a closed-choice menú — without a booking you will not get in; Saturday's menú climbs to ~€19.50, past the bargain line, and evenings are à-la-carte author-tapas territory, not menú money.
iconic_orders: Patates braves 8de7 — The calling card, rebuilt the house's own way — order them alongside whatever the menú says.; Arròs del dia — The menú's rice sittings are the ones regulars book the room out for — the house itself bills its 'arrossos memorables'.; Canelons — Old-school canelons under gratinated béchamel — grandmother territory given the bistro finish.; Menú de migdia — Three firsts, three seconds, closed choice — homestyle-modern, plated with more care than the bill implies; ~€15 weekdays.

name: Tornés (Pastisseria Tornés)
recognition: Listed in Girona city council's official catalogue of Establiments Emblemàtics — the city's register of protected historic shops.
what_to_order: The dessert buffet — individual portions of the day's house pastry, included in a €14.50-odd menú; the finish is the point.
notes: Tue–Sat 7:30–20:00, menú 13:00–16:00; Sun/holidays 8:00–13:30, no menú. Closed Mon.
verdict: The only menú del dia in Girona that ends at a 60-year-old pastry counter — a dessert buffet stocked by the family's own obrador, for less money than most old-town starters.
caveat: A south-Eixample shopping street with zero old-town romance — and the €14.50/€16.50 stickers were last firmly dated 2025 and earlier, so budget €15–17 and confirm; no menú on Sundays, closed Mondays.
iconic_orders: Menú equilibrat — Three firsts, three seconds — steamed, oven-baked or griddled by design, built so you arrive at dessert with intent.; Dessert buffet — Individual portions of the day's house pastry, included in the menú — the reason the lunch exists at all.; Xuixo — Girona's cream-filled, sugar-crusted fried pastry, made in the house obrador — take one for the walk back.; Xocolata desfeta — House-made drinking chocolate, thick enough to stand a spoon in — the granja tradition kept honest.

name: Xocolateria L'Antiga
recognition: Listed in the Ajuntament de Girona's Catàleg d'Establiments Emblemàtics — one of the city's oldest bar-cafeterias, trading since 1916
what_to_order: Xocolata desfeta amb melindros — near-black, spoon-coating, under the arcades
notes: Own site: Mon–Sat 7:00–21:00, no Sunday hours published; hostaleria listing: Mon–Fri 7:00–21:00, Sat 8:00–21:00, Sun 16:00–21:00 with mornings closed out of season; council registry: Mon–Sat 8:00–21:00 — sources differ, confirm Sundays
verdict: The nearest thing to a best-in-city call the record allows — Time Out names it 'la millor (o gairebé) xocolata desfeta de Girona' (2018), and the room has been thickening it under the same arcades since 1916.
caveat: Come for chocolate, coca and entrepans, not coffee-geekery or brunch — the rest of the offer is deliberately plain, and Sunday hours are unreliable (the three published schedules disagree; its own site lists none).
iconic_orders: Xocolata desfeta amb melindros — Poured near-black and thick enough to coat the spoon; the sponge fingers are for dragging through, not polite dunking; Xocolata suïssa — The same desfeta buried under a cap of whipped cream — richer than anything has a right to be at 8 am; Coca casolana — Home-baked and grandmother-plain — built for the cup, not the camera; Entrepà de fuet amb pa de pagès — Coarse fuet on country bread — 'boníssim', the savoury order Time Out singles out

name: Ca l'Àngel
neighborhood: Melianta, Fontcoberta — 15 min north of Girona (Pla de l'Estany)
address: Ctra. de Banyoles a Figueres, 29, 17833 Fontcoberta (urb. Melianta)
recognition: ElNacional.cat (January 2025): 'el millor esmorzar de forquilla del Pla de l'Estany' — a sensational roadside bar
what_to_order: Cap i pota at breakfast — the forquilla litmus test, with bread and a glass of red
notes: Mon–Fri 6:00–16:30, Sat 6:00–12:00; closed Sundays and holidays (official site, accessed July 2026 — ElNacional gave 6:30–16:00 in January 2025)
verdict: The best esmorzar de forquilla within a 20-minute drive of Girona — ElNacional.cat crowned it the finest in the Pla de l'Estany (2025), and the 6 am opening tells you exactly who it feeds.
caveat: It is a roadside bar in a residential estate, not a pretty room — Catalan-first, function over decor, closed Sundays and holidays with Saturday shutting at noon, and you need a car; arrive hungry and early, this is stew before 9 am.
iconic_orders: Cap i pota — Gelatinous, paprika-glossed head-and-trotter stew — wobbling, sticky-lipped, and on the table before most of Girona has had coffee; Botifarra amb mongetes — Grill-charred sausage over creamy white beans slicked with the pan fat; Mandonguilles amb bolets — Meatballs in a woodland-dark wild-mushroom sauce — the house stews are the reputation; Estofat de peix amb patates i pèsols — A briny fish stew with potatoes and peas, served at breakfast without irony; Xuixo a la brasa amb ratafia — Girona's cream-filled pastry re-crisped over the grill and flambéed with ratafia — the one flourish in an otherwise sober house

name: Federal Cafè
neighborhood: Barri Vell — Carrer de la Força (the Call)
recognition: Featured in Time Out's 'Els millors esmorzars de Girona' — nine egg preparations and its cosmopolitan morning room called out
what_to_order: Eggs Benedict under silky hollandaise, eaten on the shaded Força patio
notes: Daily from ~8:30 (recent listings give 8:30–16:00); older directories run to 23:00 and Time Out serves brunch 9:00–13:00 — closing time conflicts across sources, confirm with the café (872 264 515)
verdict: The fullest brunch menu inside the old walls — nine egg dishes and burgers before noon, a range neither La Fàbrica (bowls, toast, bagels) nor Espresso Mafia (sweet things only) attempts.
caveat: This is the Girona outpost of a Barcelona-born group with branches from Madrid to Palma — you come for Antipodean comfort done properly, not for Girona terroir; and its published hours exist in at least three conflicting versions.
iconic_orders: Eggs Benedict — Poached just-runny under silky hollandaise — the order the listings keep coming back to; Avocado toast — On rye, sharpened with lemon and black sesame; Halloumi burger — Brunch that eats like lunch — squeaky halloumi in a proper bun, one of the morning hamburgers Time Out flags; Flat white — Silky, to Antipodean spec — the founding argument of the whole group

name: Sunset Jazz Club
neighborhood: Barri Vell — Pou Rodó / Sant Feliu
address: Carrer de Jaume Pons i Martí, 12, 17004 Girona
recognition: Listed by DownBeat magazine among the best jazz clubs in the world, alongside Barcelona's Jamboree — reported February 2026
what_to_order: ★ The Saturday 22:00 second set, glass in hand, under the stone vault
notes: Posted spring-2026 hours: Mon, Tue, Thu, Sun and holidays 19:00–01:30; Fri–Sat and holiday eves 19:00–03:00; no Wednesday listed. July–Aug: Thu outdoor sets 20:00 + 21:30, Sat indoor gigs 22:00 — check the programme.
verdict: After midnight Girona's choice is this or a rumba bar — and only one of the two is on a DownBeat list; if you need food rather than music with your last drink, this card is not your answer and nothing in the city is.
caveat: Nothing to eat beyond the glass — no kitchen at all; and it is a ~50-cap room sustained by some 120 dues-paying members, so on name nights arrive at doors or you will be listening from the lane. Wednesday is absent from the posted hours — check before crossing town.
iconic_orders: Copa at the vault bar — a slow drink through the second set is the whole offer — take it under the stone ceiling and don't ask for a menu; Jazz a la Fresca first drink — the summer outdoor sets are free with an obligatory first drink — a €5 supplement applies only if weather moves the gig indoors (programme, 2026)

name: König Sandwiches
recognition: Ara/Mengem profile: 'the Girona restaurant that sells more braves than waters'
what_to_order: ★ Patates braves — the group's best-selling item, ahead of every drink line including water (Ara, 2024)
notes: Daily 9:30–23:00; Fri–Sat until 23:30 (own site, July 2026).
verdict: Order the braves at 22:45 — if a plate of crisp cubes that outsells water across an entire restaurant group doesn't read as dinner after service, nothing in Girona will at that hour; judge it as the fast-casual counter it proudly is, not as a restaurant.
caveat: Late here means 23:00 sharp, 23:30 Friday–Saturday — miss it and you are down to Zanpanzar's pintxo trays until midnight, and after that nothing hot in the city; and it is trays-and-paper fast-casual, not a place you linger.
iconic_orders: Patates braves — crisp-edged cubes under the house brava sauce — potatoes peeled and cut by hand in the group's own workshop; the best-seller across all branches; Hamburger — meat from Casa Baldiró in Banyoles, a ~50-year supplier — take it with braves alongside, not instead; Munich-style frankfurt — the founding trade — the German sausage line that started the whole dynasty in 1973; König 1973 house beer — the own-badge label — the right cold wash for brava sauce at 22:50
